title: Maya's Alphabet Parade
description: Maya turns the English alphabet into a parade of things you'd see in Praia do Sol. Each letter gets a picture, a sound, and a rhyme.
summary_pt_br: Maya transforma o alfabeto em um desfile de coisas da Praia do Sol. Cada letra tem som, imagem e rima — com foco nas que mais confundem brasileiros (G, H, J, W, Y).

scenes.text_en:
Maya is twelve. Maya is bright.
She spells her name fast — she spells it RIGHT.
'The alphabet,' Maya begins with a grin,
'is a parade. And here's where we begin!'
scenes.text_pt:
Maya tem doze. Maya é esperta.
Soletra o nome rápido, com certeza certa.
'O alfabeto,' ela começa a brincar,
'é um desfile. Vamos começar!'

scenes.text_en:
A is for Avenida — the street where we learn.
B is for Bia, who waits for her turn.
C is for Cosmo, who barks with delight.
D is for Dad, who brings lunch at night.
E is for Everyone. F is for Fun.
G is for /dʒiː/ — that's 'jee', not 'gee' — oof. This one!
scenes.text_pt:
A de Avenida — a rua do saber.
B de Bia, esperando sua vez de responder.
C de Cosmo, latindo a correr.
D de Dad (pai), trazendo o comer.
E de Everyone (todos). F de Fun (diversão).
G se fala 'djii' — atenção, irmão!

scenes.title: Letters that trip up Brazilians
scenes.body_en: These four are the most confusing: G /dʒiː/ sounds like 'jee'. J /dʒeɪ/ sounds like 'jay'. H /eɪtʃ/ starts with an 'ei' sound — never 'ah'. W /ˈdʌb.əl juː/ is called 'double U'. Y /waɪ/ sounds like the question 'why'.
scenes.body_pt: G = /djii/. J = /djei/. H começa com som de 'ei' (não 'agá'). W = 'dábol iu'. Y = 'uai' (igual ao 'why' de 'por quê'). Pratique essas cinco em voz alta.

exercise.question: Maya asks: which letter sounds like 'why'?
exercise.explanation: The letter Y is /waɪ/ — same sound as the word 'why'.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Y se pronuncia 'uai', igualzinho à pergunta 'why'.

scenes.text_en:
H is for Hello. I is for Ice.
J is for Joca — he always asks twice.
K is for Kumar, the teacher we trust.
L is for Lunchtime (it's two — we must!).
M is for Maya. N is for Nice.
O is for Ocean, where Bia finds bliss.
scenes.text_pt:
H de Hello (olá). I de Ice (gelo).
J de Joca — pergunta umas duas vezes o cabelo.
K de Kumar, o professor de confiança.
L de Lunchtime — meio-dia, sem demora!
M de Maya. N de Nice (legal).
O de Ocean, onde a Bia se dá bem, geral.

exercise.question: Spell COSMO: C, O, S, M, ___

scenes.text_en:
P is for Pizza, and Q is for Queue.
R is for Rafa — that's me, yes it's true!
S is for Surf and for Sand and for Sea.
T is for Tacos — the best? Made by me.
scenes.text_pt:
P de Pizza e Q de Queue (fila).
R de Rafa — sou eu, vila!
S de Surf, Sand, Sea (surf, areia, mar).
T de Tacos — os melhores? Só aqui, pode olhar.

exercise.explanation: The four vowel-ish letters that Brazilians most often mispronounce. Say them out loud.
exercise.hint_pt_br: A = 'ei', E = 'ii', I = 'ai', Y = 'uai'. Repita em voz alta.

scenes.text_en:
U is for Uncle. V is for View.
W is 'double-you' — a strange letter, it's true.
X is for X-ray, Y is for Yes.
Z is for Zebra — and end of the press!
That's all twenty-six. Let's spell something small.
C-O-S-M-O. That's Cosmo. That's ALL.
scenes.text_pt:
U de Uncle (tio). V de View (vista).
W é 'double-you' — letra meio trapista.
X de X-ray (raio-X), Y de Yes.
Z de Zebra — fim da apresentação, assim pois!
Vinte e seis letras. Vamos soletrar algo com clareza?
C-O-S-M-O. É Cosmo. Chegamos à sobremesa!

exercise.question: How is 'HELLO' spelled out loud?
options: Both A and B are natural
exercise.explanation: Letter-by-letter OR 'double L' for repeated letters — both sound natural.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Pode soletrar letra por letra OU usar 'double' para letras repetidas. Os dois são naturais.

scenes.title: Spelling your name
scenes.pt_summary: Soletrando nomes em inglês — atenção nas letras difíceis G/J, E/I, V/W, Y.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Hello. Please tell me your full name.
turns.pt: Olá. Por favor, diga seu nome completo.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Could you spell your first name, please?
turns.pt: Pode soletrar seu primeiro nome, por favor?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: M. A. R. I. A. Maria.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Thank you. And your last name?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: S. I. L. V. A. Silva.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Great. Could you also spell 'Brazil' for me?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: B. R. A. Z. I. L. Brazil.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: You are welcome. See you next class.
turns.pt: De nada. Até a próxima aula.

scenes.title: Listening story — Maya's Alphabet Parade
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça a situação com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que você entendeu — resumo, detalhes, seus pensamentos. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write in English what you heard. A short summary is fine. Mention who spoke, what they said, and what you think about the situation.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que você ouviu. Pode ser um resumo curto. Diga quem falou, o que disseram e o que você achou.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In this story, we meet Mr. Kumar and a student. Maya turns the English alphabet into a parade of things you'd see in Praia do Sol. Each letter gets a picture, a sound, and a rhyme. Listen carefully and think about what each person says.

paragraphs.speaker: Mr. Kumar
paragraphs.en: Hello. Please tell me your full name.

paragraphs.speaker: Mr. Kumar
paragraphs.en: Could you spell your first name, please?

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: M. A. R. I. A. Maria.

paragraphs.speaker: Mr. Kumar
paragraphs.en: Thank you. And your last name?

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: S. I. L. V. A. Silva.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: That is the end of our short scene with Mr. Kumar. Think about what you heard and how the conversation felt.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Maya's Alphabet Parade". Maya turns the English alphabet into a parade of things you'd see in Praia do Sol. Each letter gets a picture, a sound, and a rhyme. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Maya's Alphabet Parade". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Maya's Alphabet Parade'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Maya's Alphabet Parade".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
